reason: 促使 means to cause/prompt/urge someone to do something — stronger and more directive than 'encourage'; 'to prompt' is more accurate

new: You can store your luggage here.
reason: 'deposit' is unnatural English for luggage; 存放 in this context means to store/leave for safekeeping

old: She got dressed up very beautifully.
reason: 'got dressed up very beautifully' is awkward; 'dressed up beautifully' is more natural English

old: A large quantity of tourists came to this city.
new: A large number of tourists came to this city.
reason: 'a large quantity of' is for uncountable items; for people, 'a large number of' is the correct English collocation

old: A large quantity of students signed up for the competition.
new: A large number of students signed up for the competition.
reason: 'quantity' is unnatural for people; 'number' is correct

old: Company development is the top matter of great importance.
new: Company development is the top priority.
reason: 'top matter of great importance' is a clunky calque; 头等大事 is naturally rendered as 'top priority' in English

old: He substituted for me to attend the meeting.
new: He attended the meeting in my place.
reason: 'substituted for me to attend' is unnatural English; 'attended in my place' is idiomatic

old: He considered me as his friend.
new: He regarded me as his friend.
reason: 'considered as' is non-standard; 当成 means to regard/treat as, so 'regarded as' is correct

old: Don't consider a joke as real.
new: Don't treat a joke as real.
reason: 'consider ... as' is unnatural for 当成; 'treat as' is the natural English equivalent

old: She considered work as her entire life.
new: She treated work as her entire life.
reason: Same 'consider as' issue; 'treated as' better reflects 当成

old: He considers me as a friend.
new: He treats me as a friend.
reason: 'considers me as' is unnatural English; 当作 means to treat/regard as

old: Please don't consider me as an outsider.
new: Please don't treat me as an outsider.
reason: Same pattern; 'treat as' is the correct English collocation for 当作

old: She considers this place as her own home.
new: She treats this place as her own home.

reason: Row 3285 is a complete duplicate of row 3284 — same headword 登录, same definition 'to log in', same pos
做了一个洞 is unnatural collocation; 挖 (to dig) is the correct verb for making a hole in the ground

old: He made a hole in the ground.
new: He dug a hole in the ground.
reason: Matches correction to sentence_3; 'dug' is the natural English verb here
独自一个人 is redundant — 独自 already means alone; rewrite removes the redundancy

new: She lives in the city on her own.
reason: Matches corrected sentence_2; removes 'alone by herself' redundancy
